justification: B is clearly better on skill accuracy and conservatism. A bloats the list with vague meta-skills ('Full Stack Development', 'AI/LLM Integration') that are job-description-style labels rather than demonstrated technical skills, plus niche items like 'EDF parsing' and 'Clerk JWT' that are very peripheral. A also includes 'MNE' and 'RAG' and 'Vector Databases' and 'Prompt Engineering' — some of these are real but the total count of 22 is excessive for a student with ~1 year of experience. B's 13 skills are well-calibrated, accurate, and all clearly demonstrated in the resume. Neither output extracts project classifications or impact highlights as structured fields, so both score neutral (3) on those dimensions. B's 'student' experience_level is also more accurate per the expected behavior guidelines.
